meaning: The speed at which a piece of music is played.
example: The conductor asked the orchestra to play at a faster tempo.

meaning: A regular, repeated pattern of sounds or movements in music.
example: She clapped her hands to keep up with the lively rhythm of the drums.

meaning: A sequence of single notes that forms the main tune of a song.
example: The singer has a beautiful voice, and the melody of the song is easy to remember.

meaning: The combination of different musical notes played or sung at the same time to produce a pleasing sound.
example: The two singers sang in perfect harmony, making the song sound rich and full.

meaning: A group of three or more musical notes played together at the same time.
example: He learned how to play a basic C-major chord on the guitar.

meaning: A symbol placed at the beginning of a musical staff to show the pitch of the written notes.
example: Flute players read music written in the treble clef.

meaning: The set of five horizontal lines and four spaces on which musical notes are written.
example: The sheet music shows notes placed on different lines of the staff.

meaning: How high or low a sound or musical note is.
example: She can sing very high notes with perfect pitch.

meaning: The basic unit of time and rhythm in music; the steady pulse of a song.
example: The kids tapped their feet to the steady beat of the music.

meaning: A series of musical notes arranged in order of rising or falling pitch.
example: Beginning piano students spend a lot of time practicing scales.

meaning: The difference in pitch between two musical notes.
example: The interval between C and G is a perfect fifth.

meaning: The level of volume (loudness or softness) in a piece of music.
example: The sheet music had markings for dynamics, telling the pianist when to play softly.

meaning: A group of notes or a scale that forms the basis of a piece of music.
example: This song is written in the key of G major.

meaning: A small section of music containing a specific number of beats, separated by vertical lines on a staff.
example: The clarinet player missed a note in the second measure of the song.

meaning: A gradual increase in the loudness or volume of a piece of music.
example: The music built to a dramatic crescendo before the final chorus.

meaning: A gradual decrease in the loudness or volume of a piece of music.
example: The song ended with a quiet decrescendo, fading out slowly.

meaning: A device that makes a regular ticking sound to help musicians practice playing at a steady speed.
example: He used a metronome to keep his timing accurate while playing the piano.

meaning: The distance between two notes where the higher note has exactly twice the frequency of the lower note.
example: She sang the chorus again, but this time an octave higher.

meaning: A temporary shifting of the regular musical accent, placing stress on the weak beats.
example: Jazz music often uses syncopation to create a catchy, unexpected rhythm.

meaning: The system of organizing music around a central note or key.
example: The sad tonality of the piece suggests it is written in a minor key.

meaning: A piece of music or a performance by a single singer or instrument.
example: The electric guitarist played an amazing solo in the middle of the song.

meaning: A song or musical performance by two people.
example: The romantic song was performed as a duet by a male and female singer.

meaning: A group of three musicians or singers performing together.
example: The jazz trio consisted of a pianist, a drummer, and a bass player.

meaning: A group of four musicians or singers performing together.
example: A classical string quartet usually has two violins, a viola, and a cello.

meaning: A group of musicians, actors, or dancers who perform together.
example: The school brass ensemble rehearses every Wednesday afternoon.

meaning: An emphasis or extra force placed on a particular note or beat.
example: The music sheet has a little symbol showing an accent on the first note of each measure.

meaning: Numbers written at the beginning of a musical piece showing how many beats are in each measure.
example: A time signature of 4/4 means there are four quarter beats in each measure.

meaning: A musical symbol that lowers the pitch of a note by one half step.
example: To play a B-flat, you need to press a different key than for a regular B.

meaning: A musical symbol that raises the pitch of a note by one half step.
example: The key signature has three sharps, meaning those notes must be played higher.

meaning: The act of composing or performing music on the spot without any preparation or written music.
example: His performance was full of clever improvisation, making each concert unique.

meaning: A public performance of music, typically given by several musicians or a band.
example: We bought tickets to see our favorite band perform live in concert next month.

meaning: A musical performance given by a single soloist or a small group of students.
example: My little sister was nervous before her first piano recital at school.

meaning: A practice session where actors, dancers, or musicians prepare for a public performance.
example: The band had a five-hour rehearsal to make sure they knew all the songs.

meaning: The group of people who gather to watch and listen to a show, concert, or play.
example: The audience was completely quiet during the violin performance.

meaning: Approval or praise shown by clapping the hands.
example: The singer thanked the audience as loud applause filled the theater.

meaning: An extra song or performance requested by the audience at the end of a concert.
example: The crowd clapped and cheered until the band returned to play one final encore.

meaning: A raised platform in a theater or concert hall where performers act, dance, or play music.
example: The actors walked onto the stage and the lights went up.

meaning: The area behind the stage in a theater, containing dressing rooms and offices for the performers and crew.
example: We got special passes to meet the band backstage after the concert.

meaning: A device used to make quiet sounds or voices louder, or to record them.
example: The singer held the microphone close to her mouth so everyone could hear.

meaning: An electronic device used to increase the volume of musical instruments like electric guitars.
example: The guitarist plugged his instrument into a loud amplifier.

meaning: A test of sound equipment before a performance to ensure the volume and balance are correct.
example: The band arrived at the venue early for their afternoon soundcheck.

meaning: A list of songs that a musician or band plans to perform during a concert.
example: The band decided to change their setlist to include a few slower songs.

meaning: A place where an organized event, such as a concert or sporting match, takes place.
example: The city's stadium is a popular venue for major music festivals.

meaning: A single performance by a musician or group of musicians, often for money.
example: The jazz band has a gig at a local restaurant this Friday night.

meaning: An orchestral piece of music played at the beginning of an opera or musical show.
example: The theater went dark as the orchestra began playing the overture.

meaning: A short break between the parts of a play, opera, or concert.
example: During the fifteen-minute intermission, we bought some snacks and water.

meaning: An afternoon performance of a play, musical, or movie.
example: Tickets for the matinee are usually cheaper than the evening show.

meaning: The sides of a theater stage out of view of the audience, where actors wait to go on.
example: The actress stood in the wings, waiting for her cue to walk onto the stage.

meaning: A booklet given to theater audiences containing information about the play, actors, and creators.
example: I kept the playbill as a souvenir of my first time watching a Broadway show.

meaning: A room backstage in a theater where performers can relax when they are not on stage.
example: The guest speaker waited in the green room before being called to the stage.

meaning: The appearance of performers at the end of a show to receive applause from the audience.
example: The cast took three curtain calls because the audience would not stop clapping.

meaning: A person who interrupts a performer with rude, loud, or annoying comments.
example: Security removed a heckler who was shouting at the comedian during the show.

meaning: An actor who learns a role so they can replace the main actor if needed.
example: The understudy had to perform the main role when the lead actor fell ill.

meaning: To whisper lines or words to an actor on stage if they forget what to say next.
example: The stage manager had to prompt the actor when he forgot his next line.

meaning: The final practice of a play or show, done with costumes, makeup, lights, and music.
example: We had a successful dress rehearsal, so we feel ready for opening night.

meaning: The properties of a room or building that determine how clearly sound is heard in it.
example: This concert hall has excellent acoustics, making the instruments sound beautiful.

meaning: A long period of clapping where the audience stands up to show great praise.
example: The singer received a standing ovation for her powerful performance of the anthem.

meaning: The recorded music that accompanies a movie, play, or television show.
example: The movie was good, but the orchestral soundtrack was absolutely amazing.

meaning: Nervousness or fear felt by a person before performing in front of an audience.
example: Taking deep breaths before going on stage helped her overcome her stage fright.

meaning: A group of musicians who play music together, especially rock, pop, or jazz.
example: My friends and I formed a rock band and practice in our garage.

meaning: An object or device used to create musical sounds.
example: The piano is a versatile instrument that can play many styles of music.

meaning: Musical instruments played by hitting, shaking, or scraping them.
example: Drums, cymbals, and triangles are all part of the percussion family.

meaning: Musical instruments that make sound when the player blows air through them.
example: The flute and clarinet are common woodwind instruments used in orchestras.

meaning: Wind instruments made of metal, played by blowing air through a cup-shaped mouthpiece.
example: The trumpet and trombone are brass instruments that produce a bright, loud sound.

meaning: Instruments that make sound through vibrating strings when plucked, bowed, or hit.
example: The violin, cello, and guitar belong to the strings category.

meaning: A wooden stick with horsehair stretched along it, used to play instruments like the violin.
example: The violinist rubbed rosin on her bow before she started playing.

meaning: A thin piece of wood or plastic in the mouthpiece of some wind instruments that vibrates to make sound.
example: He had to replace the broken reed on his saxophone before the show.

meaning: The part of a wind instrument that the player puts their lips or mouth on to blow air.
example: She cleaned the mouthpiece of her trumpet to keep it hygienic.

meaning: A set of keys on a piano, organ, or synthesizer that you press to make musical sounds.
example: The synthesizer keyboard allows the player to choose different sound effects.

meaning: An electronic musical instrument that can produce a wide variety of sounds.
example: Electronic music bands use a synthesizer to create futuristic sound effects.

meaning: A wooden stick used to beat a drum to produce rhythmic sounds.
example: The drummer accidentally dropped a drumstick during the fast song.

meaning: Adjusting the pitch of an instrument to make sure it plays the correct notes.
example: Guitarists spend a few minutes tuning their guitars before a performance.

meaning: A slight, rapid shaking of a musical pitch to make the sound warmer or more expressive.
example: The singer used a gentle vibrato to add emotion to the final note.

meaning: The quality of a sound being deep, full, and echoing, lasting for a short time.
example: The wooden body of the acoustic guitar gives it a warm resonance.

meaning: The speed at which sound waves vibrate, which decides how high or low a pitch is.
example: Humans cannot hear sounds that vibrate at a very high frequency.

meaning: A musical instrument made of wooden bars of different lengths that you hit with small mallets.
example: The children enjoyed hitting the colorful bars of the toy xylophone.

meaning: A set of drums, cymbals, and other percussion instruments played together by one person.
example: A standard drum kit includes a bass drum, snare drum, and hi-hat cymbals.

meaning: One of the metal bars across the neck of a guitar that shows where to press the strings.
example: To play a G chord, place your fingers on the third fret of the guitar.

meaning: The size of a sound wave, which determines how loud a sound is.
example: Increasing the amplitude of the signal makes the music louder.

meaning: An electronic device or software tool used to adjust different sound frequencies (like bass and treble).
example: He used the equalizer on his music app to boost the bass of the song.

meaning: Music consisting of a single melody line without any harmony or background chords.
example: Early church music was monophonic, sung by a single group of voices in unison.

meaning: Music that has two or more independent melodies playing at the same time.
example: Classical piano pieces by Bach are famous for their complex, polyphonic style.

meaning: A fuzzy, harsh sound effect created by altering an electrical sound signal.
example: Rock guitarists use distortion to give their music a heavy and aggressive tone.

meaning: An effect that makes a sound seem as if it is echoing in a large, open space.
example: Adding reverb to the singer's voice made it sound like she was in a large cathedral.

meaning: A high, screeching sound made when sound from a speaker enters a microphone and is amplified again.
example: The singer stepped too close to the speaker, causing a loud feedback noise.

meaning: A person who stands in front of an orchestra or choir and directs them using hand gestures or a baton.
example: The conductor raised his baton, and the orchestra began to play.

meaning: A large group of musicians playing different instruments together, usually classical music.
example: The city's symphony orchestra has over eighty talented musicians.

meaning: An organized group of singers who perform together, especially in a church or concert hall.
example: The school choir sang beautiful holiday songs for the community.

meaning: Music printed on sheets of paper, showing a musician the notes they need to play.
example: She placed the sheet music on the piano and started practicing.

meaning: Music played to support or partner a main singer or soloist.
example: The singer performed a quiet ballad with a simple piano accompaniment.

meaning: The sequence of steps and movements in a dance, or the art of planning them.
example: The musical had excellent choreography that made the dance numbers exciting.

meaning: A serious play, movie, or show that focuses on emotional conflicts and realistic characters.
example: She prefers acting in deep dramas rather than comedies.

meaning: A funny play, movie, or show designed to make the audience laugh.
example: We watched a funny comedy at the local theater and laughed the whole time.

meaning: A serious play or story with an unhappy ending, usually involving the downfall of the main character.
example: Romeo and Juliet is a famous Shakespearean tragedy about young lovers.

meaning: An introduction to a play, poem, or story that gives the audience background information.
example: The narrator read the prologue to explain the history of the two families.

meaning: A short speech or section at the end of a play that wraps up the story.
example: In the epilogue, the actor explained what happened to the characters years later.

meaning: A long speech spoken by a single actor in a play or movie, directed to other characters or the audience.
example: The actress delivered a powerful monologue that made the audience cry.

meaning: The conversation between two or more characters in a play, movie, or book.
example: The dialogue in this movie is very natural and sounds like real life.

meaning: A speech in a play where a character speaks their thoughts aloud alone on stage, revealing their feelings to the audience.
example: Hamlet's 'To be or not to be' is the most famous soliloquy in English literature.

meaning: All the actors who perform in a play, movie, or show.
example: The director introduced the entire cast during the first day of practice.

meaning: The clothes worn by an actor to look like a particular character.
example: The historical play required actors to wear elaborate Victorian costumes.

meaning: Short for properties; the physical objects used by actors on stage during a performance.
example: The props on stage included a fake telephone, a map, and a wooden sword.

meaning: An instruction in the text of a play that tells actors how to move or behave on stage.
example: The stage direction in the script said: '(Exit stage left, crying)'.

meaning: The written text of a play or movie containing the lines for the actors and stage directions.
example: The actors highlighted their lines in the script to help them memorize them.

meaning: The painted backgrounds and large structures on a stage that represent the setting of a play.
example: The crew worked hard to build realistic forest scenery for the show.

meaning: An artistic style of dance performed to music using precise, graceful movements.
example: She took ballet classes to improve her balance, posture, and grace.

meaning: Short for improvisation; performing without a script, making up actions and lines as you go.
example: The actors played an improv game where they had to pretend to be in a spaceship.

meaning: A movement of a part of the body, especially a hand or head, to express an idea or emotion.
example: An open gesture like spreading your arms can show warmth and welcoming.

meaning: A short performance by an actor, dancer, or musician to show their talent and try to get a role.
example: He practiced his monologue for weeks to prepare for the theater audition.

meaning: A person who works behind the scenes in a theater to move scenery and handle equipment.
example: The stagehands quickly changed the set during the brief blackout.

meaning: A strong beam of light pointed at a specific area or performer on a stage.
example: The spotlight followed the lead singer as she walked across the stage.

meaning: A room backstage where actors change into their costumes and apply their makeup.
example: The lead actress has a private dressing room near the main stage.

meaning: A person who writes plays for the theater.
example: Shakespeare is considered one of the greatest playwrights in history.

meaning: The main character or hero in a play, story, or movie.
example: The protagonist of the story struggles to save his village from a dragon.

meaning: A character who opposes, fights, or competes with the main character in a play or story.
example: The evil stepmother is the primary antagonist in the Cinderella story.

meaning: The precise planning of where actors should stand and move on stage during a scene.
example: The director spent the afternoon working on the blocking for the final scene.

meaning: A rapid, complete turn of the body on one foot or tip of the toe, especially in ballet.
example: The dancer performed a flawless pirouette and landed gracefully on beat.

meaning: A style of dance in which shoes with metal plates on the soles are used to make rhythmic tapping sounds.
example: He practiced tap dance to improve his rhythm and footwork.

meaning: The art of using silent gestures and body movements to tell a story or portray a character without speaking.
example: The street mime pretended to be trapped inside an invisible glass box.

meaning: A signal (like a word, action, or sound) that tells a performer it is time to say or do something.
example: The ringing phone was the actor's cue to walk onto the stage.
